cuisine: Chinese / East and Southeast Asian styles
description: A rice dish made by stir-frying cooked rice with aromatics, vegetables, seasonings, and often egg or protein. It is commonly prepared with day-old rice for better texture.
core_ingredients: Aromatics such as garlic or onion
preparation_steps: Use cooked rice that is cooled and separated.
Cook aromatics and any protein first.
Add rice and toss over high heat until heated through and slightly dry.
Season and finish with egg, spring onion, or other garnishes if using.

nutrition.protein: Low to moderate; higher with egg or added protein

health_advice.benefits: Can be a practical one-pan meal that includes vegetables and protein.
health_advice.risks: Restaurant versions can be high in oil and sodium. Food-safety issues can occur if cooked rice is stored improperly before reheating.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate oil, add plenty of vegetables, and cool and refrigerate cooked rice safely before reuse.

dietary_notes.note: Vegetarian and vegan versions are common. Gluten-free versions require gluten-free soy sauce or tamari and attention to sauces.

allergens: Possible shellfish if made with shrimp
